items.title: [正面] With over 600 hours and maybe even more alongside ...
items.summary:
With over 600 hours and maybe even more alongside mobile (yes this game is crossplatform)
Do me a favor and press download while reading.

Its a REAL diamond in the rough in the gacha space.

A Lovecraft- no a cosmic horror inspired world is not one you see in gacha games. But i won't go into details about the story and art as i think you should check it yourself.
What i will talk about is the gameplay

First and foremost the combat is pretty much Slay the spire 1/2, but gacha.
Second, the game is made by a small studio, their number is unknown but can be considered 20 at MOST, so updates to the game do come out slower than other games.

Game has a stamina system yet it's not used to gate you from playing the story which is very good for people who want to just play all day with no worry of being blueballed before a climactic stage. Along with being able to sweep farming stages once you clear them once. Very timesaving.



Now the next important part, the gacha. It's very generous as you are guaranteed a unit at worst 30 pulls, which is very low amount of pulls needed and you accumilate the premium currency very VERY fast. 
BUT there is a big BUT, the game does use a dupe system. So not many characters are good at their base copy, the second.third and fourth copy unlocks more parts of the kit with some being manditory as to not have a painful experience, looking at you Kathigu-ra (she needs 4 total copies), not to mention you can lose the 50/50 twice, resulting at worst odds, 90 pulls for one copy of the unit you want.

Good news is that it's somewhat alleviated by a shop, every month you are able to get two items that give you a free dupe, one for a unit and one for a wheel (weapon), you can also hoard these items.
To add on, not every standard unit is bad like other gacha games do, and in addition the older limited units get added onto the standard banner as low rate drop units so you dont get spooked by the same faces, and some of these older limiteds are STILL godlike to this day.

TLDR: the gacha is good and generous, but quite vertical in terms of unit power.



Next up, gearing system
The units use Wheels of Destiny (Weapons), you can carry 1 and later 2, with even the ability to carry 2 Limited Wheels at a much much later point in the game.
With effects so broad many units can just yoink someone else's Wheel and just use it (ofc supports would rather not use crit damage wheels but who am i to stop you).

The second part of the gearing system is Covenants.
Those are basically your artefacts ala Genshin, Chaos zero nightmare and others etc.
And just like the Wheels, covenants are very universal with effects so broad you can just slap it onto just about anyone...minus the crit dmg sets on supports.
Now you would think with 50+ units you would have to make multiple sets for characters, HA, no.
You are able to create a set and then use that set on anyone from another loadout of units without needing to fidget around, meaning if you were to pick the 4 most universal sets in the game you can never touch this system after you are done building out the stats.
Speaking of stats, the stats are very different from the norm, to name a few, there is Death Resistance, Realm Mastery, Keyflare and many more that are usable on just about any team. Some obviously more useful than others. These stats are rerollable with little effort so if you were to get the main stat of a piece you want but has garbage substats, just roll.

Second TLDR: Gearing is painless



Now for the last part, THE ENDGAME (D-Zone)
Endgame is rather simple, there is no turn limit or timer, just beat the stage with whatever means you see fit.
This alone is quite refreshing since that opens the possibilty of just playing a very stall heavy team that will kill the enemy from old age.
It consists of 5 stages, in which once you use a team and their wheels on one stage, they become locked/used up.
Meaning to be able to clear all 5 stages you need to have at the bare minimum 20 units. Each stage has a difficulty score which for a new player id suggest stick to lv 1-3 as the rewards are frontloaded in the begining, so you dont need to worry about having the most meta team if you just want the pull currency and then dip.
And returning back to the covenants for a bit, you are able to reuse the covenants you used for your teams in another one since those do not get locked from use.



The game also boasts several others modes like the Slay the spire focused one "Hectic Skybound Rail", which is currently more of a concept for now.

Traphase a PVP mode (yeah, pvp in gacha), dupes dont matter in this mode, any character, wheel and posse you own is usable here, but if you were to want to use the entire roster, you can pay for a monthly that unlocks everything in the mode to play without owning the characters. It is a gameplay mode that was present in the Developer's previous game called My Turn (its a card game)

And last one is P-Dive, which is a weekly dailyboard where certain units get buffed stats for the week and you play for highscore.
These 3 modes are there to fill your time when you are tired of the usual and even there you can accumulate pull currency.



Now i will go into the community, its the same as every other gacha game community, some good some bad, but for the most part, cool.
Since game is very niche there aren't many mainstream people playing or talking about the game so most of the gacha "normies" don't even know this game exists or have people that harass the devs or va's 
Speaking of the Devs, they give out gift codes when the community hits a new milestone in people, which is very often as of recently. Thank you THPDom, Pseychie, CipherRei, Crit Hit and many others.

Before hopping into the community and asking for help and such, please do try to do it on your own, the game is meant to be difficult but finding eureka moments is what will allow you to push yourself through beings beyond your mortal comprehension.
Do check out the main discord server after you have cleared atleast chapter 5 of the game, thanksies.

May the silver key guide us.

items.title: [负面] Once again I'm trying out new stuff and wondering ...
items.summary:
Once again I'm trying out new stuff and wondering why it got that positive reviews ~.~
Yes, it has unique aesthetics but..that's it?

- another gacha that starts fully voiced and you think 'Oh cool, that's nice!' just to destroy that thought right after bc it's only text then
- no auto-combat for those who want as little interaction as possible with turn based card stuff
- turn based ''tactical'' combat with lots of stuff to learn but almost no tutorial
- ''exploration'' on a tiny card board with very little meaning, can't go where you click (have to go one by one even if you've been on these tiles before)
- almost no rewards for starting out
- 0815 UI that's just overwhelming and showering you with stuff to do with little to no explanation and progression
- character trials are maxed characters. My first trial took me what felt like 15 min and yeah, you get a full pull out of it but gosh I almost fell asleep. If that's a glimpse of endgame gameplay, then no thanks.
- story could be nice but I'm so tired of playing ''the only one that can save the world'' 
- some fanservice and mostly female characters 
- very early popup to buy something for real money

If the world only sees one direction, I'm delighted to show you something different.

items.title: [正面] Lovecraftian horror Slay the Spire in a free to pl...
items.summary:
Lovecraftian horror Slay the Spire in a free to play package.  If you can navigate the truly dizzying number of currencies this game throws at you, it's a great game.  Cool character designs, enjoyable gameplay loop.  A few dozen hours in, it doesn't seem to be too-too predatory in its free-to-playness.

I dunno.  I unlocked everything in Slay the Spire 2 already and found myself wishing I had a game I could mess around with for a longer period of time and lo and behold, the monkey's paw did curl...

items.title: [正面] Its good game very good art style, but i think the...
items.summary: Its good game very good art style, but i think the gameplay for one stage for beginner or first try takes long time. And yeah i prefer to buy a game like Slay the Spire - inspired gameplay and not to spend in microtransactions, and the most annoy thing my ping is 400 i can't play duo to lack of support the servers.
